entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The crop species being studied for rust resistance.

entities.name: GROUNDNUT RUST
entities.description: The fungal pathogen causing rust disease in groundnuts.

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The environmental variable tested to observe its effect on rust components.

entities.name: INCUBATION PERIOD
entities.description: The time interval between infection and the appearance of symptoms.

entities.name: INFECTION FREQUENCY
entities.description: The rate or frequency of rust infection on the plant tissue.

entities.name: LESION DIAMETER
entities.description: The size of the rust lesions formed on the leaves.

entities.name: LEAF AREA DAMAGE
entities.description: The extent of leaf tissue destroyed by the rust infection.

entities.name: PUSTULE RUPTURE
entities.description: The process of rust pustules breaking through the leaf epidermis.

entities.name: SPORULATION
entities.description: The production of spores by the rust fungus.

entities.name: DETACHED LEAVES
entities.description: The experimental technique of using leaves separated from the plant for infection studies.

entities.name: RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of a genotype to limit the development of rust.

entities.name: SUSCEPTIBILITY
entities.description: The lack of resistance to rust, allowing the pathogen to develop fully.

entities.name: NC AC 17090
entities.description: A specific groundnut genotype mentioned for its unique response to temperature.

relationships.description: Influences the length of the incubation period
Influences the size of the lesions
Influences the extent of leaf area damage
Influences the percentage of pustules that rupture
Influences the amount of spore production
Causes rust disease in the crop
Used as a medium to study the development of rust
Associated with a longer incubation period
Associated with a reduced number of ruptured pustules
Associated with reduced leaf area damage
Is a specific genotype of groundnut
Shows an exceptional response to temperature regarding pustule rupture and sporulation
